016package org.consensusj.bitcoin.jsonrpc;
017
018import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JavaType;
019import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode;
020import org.bitcoinj.base.AddressParser;
021import org.bitcoinj.base.BitcoinNetwork;
022import org.bitcoinj.base.Network;
023import org.bitcoinj.base.ScriptType;
024import org.bitcoinj.params.BitcoinNetworkParams;
025import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.AddressInfo;
026import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.LoadWalletResult;
027import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.Outpoint;
028import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.SignedRawTransaction;
029import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.UnspentOutput;
030import org.bitcoinj.core.Block;
031import org.bouncycastle.util.encoders.Hex;
032import org.consensusj.bitcoin.jsonrpc.bitcoind.BitcoinConfFile;
033import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcStatusException;
034import org.bitcoinj.base.Address;
035import org.bitcoinj.base.Coin;
036import org.bitcoinj.crypto.ECKey;
037import org.bitcoinj.base.Sha256Hash;
038import org.bitcoinj.core.Transaction;
039import org.bitcoinj.core.TransactionOutPoint;
040import org.bitcoinj.core.TransactionOutput;
041import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcTransport;
042import org.slf4j.Logger;
043import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
044
045import javax.net.ssl.SSLContext;
046import java.io.IOException;
047import java.math.BigInteger;
048import java.net.URI;
049import java.time.Instant;
050import java.util.HashMap;
051import java.util.List;
052import java.util.Map;
053import java.util.stream.Collectors;
054
055/**
056 * Extended Bitcoin JSON-RPC Client with added convenience methods.
057 *
058 * This class adds extra methods that aren't 1:1 mappings to standard
059 * Bitcoin API RPC methods, but are useful for many common use cases -- specifically
060 * the ones we ran into while building integration tests.
061 */
062public class BitcoinExtendedClient extends BitcoinClient {
063    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(BitcoinExtendedClient.class);
064
065    public static final Address DEFAULT_REGTEST_MINING_ADDRESS = AddressParser.getDefault().parseAddress("mwQA8f4pH23BfHyy4zf8mgAyeNu5uoy6GU");
066    private static final BigInteger NotSoPrivatePrivateInt = new BigInteger(1, Hex.decode("180cb41c7c600be951b5d3d0a7334acc7506173875834f7a6c4c786a28fcbb19"));
067    private static final String RegTestMiningAddressLabel = "RegTestMiningAddress";
068    public static final String REGTEST_WALLET_NAME = "consensusj-regtest-wallet";
069    private boolean regTestWalletInitialized = false;
070    private /* lazy */ Address regTestMiningAddress;
071
072    public final Coin stdTxFee = Coin.valueOf(10000);
073    public final Coin stdRelayTxFee = Coin.valueOf(1000);
074    public final Integer defaultMaxConf = 9999999;
075    public final long stdTxFeeSatoshis = stdTxFee.getValue();
076
077    public Coin getStdTxFee() {
078        return stdTxFee;
079    }
080
081    public Coin getStdRelayTxFee() {
082        return stdRelayTxFee;
083    }
084
085    public Integer getDefaultMaxConf() {
086        return defaultMaxConf;
087    }
088
089
090    public BitcoinExtendedClient(SSLContext sslContext, Network network, URI server, String rpcuser, String rpcpassword) {
091        super(sslContext, network, server, rpcuser, rpcpassword);
092    }
093
094    public BitcoinExtendedClient(Network network, URI server, String rpcuser, String rpcpassword) {
095        this(JsonRpcTransport.getDefaultSSLContext(), network, server, rpcuser, rpcpassword);
096    }
097
098    public BitcoinExtendedClient(URI server, String rpcuser, String rpcpassword) {
099        this(JsonRpcTransport.getDefaultSSLContext(), null, server, rpcuser, rpcpassword);
100    }
101
102    public BitcoinExtendedClient(RpcConfig config) {
103        this(config.network(), config.getURI(), config.getUsername(), config.getPassword());
104    }
105
106    /**
107     * Constructor that uses bitcoin.conf to get connection information (Incubating)
108     */
109    public BitcoinExtendedClient() {
110        this(BitcoinConfFile.readDefaultConfig().getRPCConfig());
111    }
112
113    /**
114     * Incubating: clone the client with a new base URI for a named wallet.
115     * @param walletName wallet name
116     * @param rpcUser username must be provided because it is not accessible in the parent class
117     * @param rpcPassword password must be provided because it is not accessible in the parent class
118     * @return A new client with a baseURI configured for the specified wallet name.
119     */
120    public BitcoinExtendedClient withWallet(String walletName, String rpcUser, String rpcPassword) {
121        return new BitcoinExtendedClient(this.getNetwork(), this.getServerURI().resolve("/wallet/" + walletName), rpcUser, rpcPassword);
122    }
123
124    public synchronized Address getRegTestMiningAddress() {
125        if (getNetwork() != BitcoinNetwork.REGTEST) {
126            throw new UnsupportedOperationException("Operation only supported in RegTest context");
127        }
128        if (regTestMiningAddress == null) {
129            if (!regTestWalletInitialized) {
130                initRegTestWallet();
131            }
132            // If in the future, we want to manage the keys for mined coins on the client side,
133            // we could initialize regTestMiningKey from a bitcoinj-generated ECKey or HD Keychain.
134            try {
135                ECKey notSoPrivatePrivateKey = ECKey.fromPrivate(NotSoPrivatePrivateInt, false);
136                Address address = notSoPrivatePrivateKey.toAddress(ScriptType.P2PKH, BitcoinNetwork.REGTEST);
137                AddressInfo addressInfo = getAddressInfo(address);
138                if (addressInfo.getIsmine() && !addressInfo.getIswatchonly() && addressInfo.getSolvable()) {
139                    log.warn("Address with label {} is present in server-side wallet", RegTestMiningAddressLabel);
140                    regTestMiningAddress = address;
141                } else if (getServerVersion() >= BITCOIN_CORE_VERSION_DESC_DEFAULT) {
142                    // Import known private key as descriptor wallet and rescan the blockchain for any transactions from
143                    // previous test runs, rescan shouldn't take too long on a typical RegTest chain
144                    // TODO: This pretty much works, but here is (was) also an issue with the ` confidence.depthInBlocks == 1` condition
145                    // in WalletSendSpec. We can enable this code when those are fixed.
146                    String wif = notSoPrivatePrivateKey.getPrivateKeyAsWiF(BitcoinNetwork.REGTEST);
147                    String descriptor = String.format("pkh(%s)#uv04hy66", wif);     // TODO: Fix hard-coded checksum
148                    log.warn("Adding private key descriptor {} for {} and rescanning chain", descriptor, RegTestMiningAddressLabel);
149                    JsonNode result = importDescriptor(descriptor, false, Instant.EPOCH, RegTestMiningAddressLabel);
150                    log.warn("importDescriptor result: {}", result);
151                    regTestMiningAddress = address;
152                } else {
153                    // Import known private key with label and rescan the blockchain for any transactions from
154                    // previous test runs, rescan shouldn't take too long on a typical RegTest chain
155                    log.warn("Adding private key for {} and rescanning chain", RegTestMiningAddressLabel);
156                    importPrivKey(notSoPrivatePrivateKey, RegTestMiningAddressLabel, true);
157                    regTestMiningAddress = address;
158                }
159                log.warn("Retrieved regTestMiningAddress = {}", regTestMiningAddress);
160            } catch (IOException e) {
161                log.error("Exception while checking/importing regTestMiningAddress", e);
162                throw new RuntimeException(e);
163            }
164        }
165        return regTestMiningAddress;
166    }
167
168    /**
169     * Initialize a server-side wallet for RegTest mining and test transaction funding. Creates a non-descriptor wallet
170     * with name {@link #REGTEST_WALLET_NAME} if it doesn't already exist.
171     */
172    public synchronized void initRegTestWallet() {
173        if (!regTestWalletInitialized) {
174            // Create a named wallet for RegTest (previously we used the default wallet with an empty-string name)
175            int bitcoinCoreVersion = getServerVersion();
176            try {
177                List<String> walletList = listWallets();
178                if (!walletList.contains(REGTEST_WALLET_NAME)) {
179                    createRegTestWallet(bitcoinCoreVersion, REGTEST_WALLET_NAME);
180                }
181            } catch (IOException ioe) {
182                throw new RuntimeException(ioe);
183            }
184            regTestWalletInitialized = true;
185        }
186    }
187
188    /**
189     * Create a server-side wallet suitable for RegTest mining/funding, defaulting all other parameters.
190     * @param bitcoinCoreVersion Used to select correct JSON-RPC parameters to used based on server version
191     * @param name name of wallet to create
192     */
193    private void createRegTestWallet(int bitcoinCoreVersion, String name) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
194        LoadWalletResult result = (bitcoinCoreVersion >= BITCOIN_CORE_VERSION_DESC_DEFAULT)
195                    // Use newer parameters and create descriptor wallet (if supported), for older Bitcoin Core use older params
196                    ? createWallet(name, false, false, null, null, true, null, null)
197                    : createWallet(name, false, false, null, null);
198
199        if (result.getWarning() == null || result.getWarning().isEmpty()) {
200            log.info("Created REGTEST wallet: \"{}\"", result.getName());
201        } else {
202            log.warn("Warning creating REGTEST wallet \"{}\": {}", result.getName(), result.getWarning());
203        }
204    }
205
206    /**
207     * Generate blocks and funds (RegTest only)
208     *
209     * Use this to generate blocks and receive the block reward in {@code this.regTestMiningAddress}
210     * which can the be used to fund transactions in RegTest mode.
211     *
212     * @param numBlocks Number of blocks to mine
213     * @return list of block hashes
214     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException something broke
215     * @throws IOException something broke
216     */
217    public List<Sha256Hash> generateBlocks(int numBlocks) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
218        return this.generateToAddress(numBlocks, getRegTestMiningAddress());
219    }
220
221    /**
222     * Calculate the subsidy portion block reward for a given height on the current network
223     * @param height the height at which to calculate the mining subsidy
224     * @return mining subsidy
225     */
226    public Coin getBlockSubsidy(int height) {
227        BitcoinNetworkParams params = (BitcoinNetworkParams) BitcoinNetworkParams.of(getNetwork());
228        return params.getBlockInflation(height);
229    }
230
231    /**
232     * Returns information about a block at index provided.
233     *
234     * Uses two RPCs: {@code getblockhash} and then {@code getblock}.
235     *
236     * @param index The block index
237     * @return The information about the block
238     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
239     * @throws IOException network error
240     */
241    public Block getBlock(int index) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
242        Sha256Hash blockHash = getBlockHash(index);
243        return getBlock(blockHash);
244    }
245
246    /**
247     * Clears the memory pool and returns a list of the removed transactions.
248     *
249     * Note: this is a customized command, which is currently not part of Bitcoin Core.
250     * See https://github.com/OmniLayer/OmniJ/pull/72[Pull Request #72] on GitHub
251     *
252     * @return A list of transaction hashes of the removed transactions
253     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status exception
254     * @throws IOException network error
255     */
256    public List<Sha256Hash> clearMemPool() throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
257        JavaType resultType = collectionTypeForClasses(List.class, Sha256Hash.class);
258        return (List<Sha256Hash>) send("clearmempool", resultType);
259    }
260
261    /**
262     * Creates a raw transaction, spending from a single address, whereby no new change address is created, and
263     * remaining amounts are returned to {@code fromAddress}.
264     * <p>
265     * Note: the transaction inputs are not signed, and the transaction is not stored in the wallet or transmitted to
266     * the network.
267     *
268     * @param fromAddress The source to spend from
269     * @param outputs The destinations and amounts to transfer
270     * @return The hex-encoded raw transaction
271     */
272    public String createRawTransaction(Address fromAddress, Map<Address, Coin> outputs) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
273        // Copy the Map (which may be immutable) and add prepare change output if needed.
274        Map<Address, Coin> outputsWithChange = new HashMap<>(outputs);
275        // Get unspent outputs via RPC
276        List<UnspentOutput> unspentOutputs = listUnspent(0, defaultMaxConf, fromAddress);
277
278        // Gather inputs as OutPoints
279        List<Outpoint> inputs = unspentOutputs.stream()
280                .map(this::unspentToOutpoint)
281                .collect(Collectors.toList());
282
283        // Calculate change
284        final long amountIn = unspentOutputs
285                .stream()
286                .map(UnspentOutput::getAmount)
287                .mapToLong(Coin::getValue)
288                .sum();
289        final long amountOut = outputs.values()
290                .stream()
291                .mapToLong(Coin::getValue)
292                .sum();
293
294        // Change is the difference less the standard transaction fee
295        final long amountChange = amountIn - amountOut - stdTxFeeSatoshis;
296        if (amountChange < 0) {
297            // TODO: Throw Exception
298            System.out.println("Insufficient funds"); // + ": ${amountIn} < ${amountOut + stdTxFee}"
299        }
300        if (amountChange > 0) {
301            // Add a change output that returns change to sending address
302            outputsWithChange.put(fromAddress, Coin.valueOf(amountChange));
303        }
304
305        // Call the server to create the transaction
306        return createRawTransaction(inputs, outputsWithChange);
307    }
308
309    /**
310     * Creates a raw transaction, sending {@code amount} from a single address to a destination, whereby no new change
311     * address is created, and remaining amounts are returned to {@code fromAddress}.
312     * <p>
313     * Note: the transaction inputs are not signed, and the transaction is not stored in the wallet or transmitted to
314     * the network.
315     *
316     * @param fromAddress The source to spent from
317     * @param toAddress The destination
318     * @param amount The amount
319     * @return The hex-encoded raw transaction
320     */
321    public String createRawTransaction(Address fromAddress, Address toAddress, Coin amount) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
322        return createRawTransaction(fromAddress, Map.of(toAddress, amount));
323    }
324
325    /**
326     * Returns the Bitcoin balance of an address (in the server-side wallet.)
327     *
328     * @param address The address
329     * @return The balance
330     */
331    public Coin getBitcoinBalance(Address address) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
332        // NOTE: because null is currently removed from the argument lists passed via RPC, using it here for default
333        // values would result in the RPC call "listunspent" with arguments [["address"]], which is invalid, similar
334        // to a call with arguments [null, null, ["address"]], as expected arguments are either [], [int], [int, int]
335        // or [int, int, array]
336        return getBitcoinBalance(address, 1, defaultMaxConf);
337    }
338
339    /**
340     * Returns the Bitcoin balance of an address (in the server-side wallet) where spendable outputs have at least
341     * {@code minConf} confirmations.
342     *
343     * @param address The address
344     * @param minConf Minimum amount of confirmations
345     * @return The balance
346     */
347    public Coin getBitcoinBalance(Address address, Integer minConf) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
348        return getBitcoinBalance(address, minConf, defaultMaxConf);
349    }
350
351    /**
352     * Returns the Bitcoin balance of an address (in the server-side wallet) where spendable outputs have at least
353     * {@code minConf} and not more than {@code maxConf} confirmations.
354     *
355     * @param address The address (must be in wallet)
356     * @param minConf Minimum amount of confirmations
357     * @param maxConf Maximum amount of confirmations
358     * @return The balance
359     */
360    public Coin getBitcoinBalance(Address address, Integer minConf, Integer maxConf) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
361        return listUnspent(minConf, maxConf, address)
362                .stream()
363                .map(UnspentOutput::getAmount)
364                .reduce(Coin.ZERO, Coin::add);
365    }
366
367    /**
368     * Sends BTC from an address to a destination, whereby no new change address is created, and any leftover is
369     * returned to the sending address.
370     *
371     * @param fromAddress The source to spent from
372     * @param toAddress   The destination address
373     * @param amount      The amount to transfer
374     * @return The transaction hash
375     */
376    public Sha256Hash sendBitcoin(Address fromAddress, Address toAddress, Coin amount) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
377        Map<Address, Coin> outputs = Map.of(toAddress, amount);
378        return sendBitcoin(fromAddress, outputs);
379    }
380
381    /**
382     * Sends BTC from an address to the destinations, whereby no new change address is created, and any leftover is
383     * returned to the sending address.
384     *
385     * @param fromAddress The source to spent from
386     * @param outputs     The destinations and amounts to transfer
387     * @return The transaction hash
388     */
389    public Sha256Hash sendBitcoin(Address fromAddress, Map<Address, Coin> outputs) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
390        String unsignedTxHex = createRawTransaction(fromAddress, outputs);
391        SignedRawTransaction signingResult = signRawTransactionWithWallet(unsignedTxHex);
392
393        boolean complete = signingResult.isComplete();
394        assert complete;
395
396        String signedTxHex = signingResult.getHex();
397        Sha256Hash txid = sendRawTransaction(signedTxHex, null, null);
398
399        return txid;
400    }
401
402    /**
403     * Create a signed transaction locally (i.e. with a client-side key.) Finds UTXOs this
404     * key can spend (assuming they are ScriptType.P2PKH UTXOs)
405     *
406     * @param fromKey Signing key
407     * @param outputs Outputs to sign
408     * @return A bitcoinj Transaction objects that is properly signed
409     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException A JSON-RPC error was returned
410     * @throws IOException            An I/O error occured
411     */
412    public Transaction createSignedTransaction(ECKey fromKey, List<TransactionOutput> outputs) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
413        Address fromAddress = fromKey.toAddress(ScriptType.P2PKH, getNetwork());
414
415        Transaction tx = new Transaction();   // Create a new transaction
416        outputs.forEach(tx::addOutput);                     // Add all requested outputs to it
417
418        // Fetch all UTXOs for the sending Address
419        List<TransactionOutput> unspentOutputs = listUnspentJ(fromAddress);
420
421        // Calculate change (units are satoshis)
422        // First sum all available UTXOs
423        final Coin amountIn = unspentOutputs.stream()
424                .map(TransactionOutput::getValue)
425                .reduce(Coin.ZERO, Coin::add);
426        // Then sum the requested outputs for this transaction
427        final Coin amountOut = outputs.stream()
428                .map(TransactionOutput::getValue)
429                .reduce(Coin.ZERO, Coin::add);
430        // Change is the difference less the standard transaction fee
431        final long amountChange = amountIn.value - amountOut.value - stdTxFeeSatoshis;
432        if (amountChange < 0) {
433            // TODO: Throw Exception
434            System.out.println("Insufficient funds"); // + ": ${amountIn} < ${amountOut + stdTxFeeSatoshis}"
435        }
436        if (amountChange > 0) {
437            // Add a change output that returns change to sending address
438            tx.addOutput(Coin.valueOf(amountChange), fromAddress);
439        }
440
441        // Add *all* UTXOs for fromAddress as inputs (this perhaps unnecessarily consolidates coins, with
442        // a higher tx fee and some loss of privacy) and sign them
443        unspentOutputs.forEach(unspent -> tx.addSignedInput(unspent, fromKey));
444        return tx;
445    }
446
447    /**
448     * Create a signed transaction locally (i.e. with a client-side key.) Finds UTXOs this
449     * key can spend (assuming they are org.bitcoinj.base.ScriptType.P2PKH UTXOs)
450     *
451     * @param fromKey   Signing key
452     * @param toAddress Destination address
453     * @param amount    Amount to send
454     * @return A bitcoinj Transaction objects that is properly signed
455     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException A JSON-RPC error was returned
456     * @throws IOException            An I/O error occured
457     */
458    public Transaction createSignedTransaction(ECKey fromKey, Address toAddress, Coin amount) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
459        List<TransactionOutput> outputs = List.of(
460                new TransactionOutput(null, amount, toAddress));
461        return createSignedTransaction(fromKey, outputs);
462    }
463
464    /**
465     * Build a list of bitcoinj {@link TransactionOutput}s using {@link BitcoinClient#listUnspent}
466     * and {@link BitcoinClient#getRawTransaction} RPCs.
467     *
468     * @param fromAddress Address to get UTXOs for
469     * @return All unspent TransactionOutputs for fromAddress
470     */
471    public List<TransactionOutput> listUnspentJ(Address fromAddress) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
472        List<UnspentOutput> unspentOutputs = listUnspent(0, defaultMaxConf, fromAddress); // RPC UnspentOutput objects
473        return unspentOutputs.stream()
474                .map(this::unspentToTransactionOutput)
475                .collect(Collectors.toList());
476    }
477
478    /**
479     * Build a list of bitcoinj {@link TransactionOutPoint}s using {@link BitcoinClient#listUnspent}.
480     *
481     * @param fromAddress Address to get UTXOs for
482     * @return All unspent TransactionOutPoints for fromAddress
483     */
484    public List<TransactionOutPoint> listUnspentOutPoints(Address fromAddress) throws JsonRpcStatusException, IOException {
485        List<UnspentOutput> unspentOutputsRPC = listUnspent(0, defaultMaxConf, fromAddress); // RPC UnspentOutput objects
486        return unspentOutputsRPC.stream()
487                .map(this::unspentToTransactionOutpoint)
488                .collect(Collectors.toList());
489    }
490    
491    /**
492     * Convert an {@link UnspentOutput} JSONRPC-POJO to a *bitcoinj* {@link TransactionOutput} (that's out-PUT).
493     * Calls {@link BitcoinClient#getRawTransaction(Sha256Hash)}
494     * for every item. Throws {@link RuntimeException} if any of those RPC calls fails.
495     *
496     * @param unspentOutput The input POJO
497     * @return The *bitcoinj* object  (that's out-PUT)
498     */
499    private TransactionOutput unspentToTransactionOutput(UnspentOutput unspentOutput) {
500        try {
501            return getRawTransaction(unspentOutput.getTxid())
502                    .getOutput(unspentOutput.getVout());
503        } catch (IOException e) {
504            throw new RuntimeException(e);
505        }
506    }
507
508    /**
509     * Convert an {@link UnspentOutput} JSONRPC-POJO to a *bitcoinj* {@link TransactionOutPoint} (that's out-POINT).
510     *
511     * @param unspentOutput The input POJO
512     * @return The *bitcoinj* object  (that's out-POINT)
513     */
514    private TransactionOutPoint unspentToTransactionOutpoint(UnspentOutput unspentOutput) {
515        return new TransactionOutPoint(unspentOutput.getVout(), unspentOutput.getTxid());
516    }
517
518    /**
519     * Convert an {@link UnspentOutput} JSONRPC-POJO to a JSONRPC-POJO {@link Outpoint} .
520     *
521     * @param unspentOutput The input UnspentOutput POJO
522     * @return the Outpoint POJO
523     */
524    private Outpoint unspentToOutpoint(UnspentOutput unspentOutput) {
525        return new Outpoint(unspentOutput.getTxid(), unspentOutput.getVout());
526    }
527}
